title: From Silent Spring to Sustainable Labs: The Historical Development and Modern Application of Green Chemistry Principles in Drug Development
description: This article traces the origins and evolution of green chemistry from its environmental roots in the 1960s to its formal establishment in the 1990s, providing a comprehensive overview for researchers, scientists, and drug development professionals. It explores the foundational 12 principles developed by Anastas and Warner, examines cutting-edge methodological applications in pharmaceutical R&D, addresses key optimization challenges, and validates the approach through industry case studies and metrics.
audience: researchers, scientists, and drug development professionals
